narration: DFS starts at one node and keeps going down one route before backing up.
callout: One possible DFS from A is A, B, D, C.

narration: BFS looks at the nearest neighbors first, so it spreads outward level by level.
decision: One possible BFS from A is A, B, C, D.

predictionPrompt.prompt: Which graph walk usually uses a queue?

choices.label: BFS
choices.feedback: Correct. BFS uses a queue to stay level by level.

choices.label: DFS
choices.feedback: Not this one. DFS usually goes deep with recursion or a stack.

narration: Because graphs can connect back to old nodes, visited memory keeps the walk from repeating forever.
callout: Visited memory says, 'We have already handled that node.'
